decision: VM command execution will be exposed as an optional nanofused API capability instead of direct CLI or tray calls into runtime tooling.
rationale: The daemon owns VM lifecycle and runtime handles; keeping exec behind the daemon preserves the API boundary for macOS and Windows clients while allowing runtimes such as Apple container to implement command execution.
alternatives: Directly shelling out to Apple container from the CLI was rejected because it bypasses nanofused and cannot work for remote API clients; implementing SSH as the primary control path was rejected because SSH depends on guest image contents and port forwarding rather than the runtime boundary itself.

decision: Runtime capability gaps will use a shared unsupported-operation sentinel and API handlers will map it to HTTP 501 with UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION.
rationale: Nanofuse now has Firecracker and Apple-container backends with different feature coverage; clients need to distinguish a valid operation unsupported by the selected runtime from daemon failures and guest command exit codes.
alternatives: Mapping unsupported runtime features to INTERNAL_ERROR/500 was rejected because it misclassifies backend capability gaps as daemon failures; hiding unsupported operations only in clients was rejected because remote clients would still need server-side enforcement.
